_doc: Single source of truth for the quasar framework test matrix. Each case declares its driver inputs (design/config/oracle/device_sources/generate_devices) and its 'kind' (oracle|smoke), which decides how a result is judged. Cells are (case x backend x os x compiler); .CI/expand_matrix.py flattens them into a GitHub Actions strategy.matrix, and run_test_case.py --case reads the SAME file so a case is declared exactly once. The CI image of a cell is ghcr.io/<owner>/quasar-ci:<os>-<backend>[-<toolkit_version>]. Backend coverage is symmetric -- every case runs on both o6 and uasdk now that the backends are at feature parity (a short per-cell exclude would capture any genuine semantic delta; there are none today). PR/nightly tiers and the UASDK-version axis are layered on top.
_nightly_os_doc: OSes that run the FULL case set (all cases x backends x toolkit versions) at nightly tier. default_os carries the per-case/per-version tiers (PR + nightly); nightly_os mirrors the whole set on the newer EL at nightly so PR stays lean. Explicit per-case extra_cells (e.g. default_design's PR alma10 smoke + clang) are preserved -- they are emitted first and win de-duplication.
_nightly_compilers_doc: Extra compiler(s) run over the FULL case set at nightly tier (compiler-portability coverage beyond the default gcc). clang on alma10/o6 -- uasdk static libs are gcc-built so clang is kept to o6 to avoid a cross-compiler-ABI variable.
_nightly_arches_doc: Extra CPU architectures run at nightly on native GitHub arm64 runners (ubuntu-24.04-arm, free for public repos) with arch-tagged images (e.g. quasar-ci:alma9-o6-arm64). 'versions' limits which uasdk toolkit versions get an arch image built; o6 + uasdk 1.7.9 on alma9 is the representative aarch64 set (the user ships aarch64 SDK builds). Same gcc/libstdc++ family as x86_64, so the framework/compat/uasak builds are portable.
